title.value: Stop Overthinking: A Survey on Efficient Reasoning for Large Language Models
title.evidence: Stop Overthinking: A Survey on Efficient Reasoning for Large Language Models

background.value: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as exceptionally powerful AI tools, demonstrating advanced capabilities in natural language understanding and complex reasoning.
background.evidence: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as exceptionally powerful AI tools, demonstrating advanced capabilities in natural language understanding and complex reasoning.

problem_statement.value: Efficient reasoning, particularly the reduction of reasoning length, offers significant benefits in such regards, providing direct cost reduction and improved feasibility for real-world deployments.
problem_statement.evidence: Efficient reasoning, particularly the reduction of reasoning length, offers significant benefits in such regards, providing direct cost reduction and improved feasibility for real-world deployments.

description.value: Model-based efficient reasoning, which focuses on optimizing full-length reasoning models into more concise variants or directly training efficient reasoning models.
description.evidence: Model-based efficient reasoning, which focuses on optimizing full-length reasoning models into more concise variants or directly training efficient reasoning models.

examples.evidence: Recent reasoning models, such as DeepSeek-R1 [56] and OpenAI o1 [137], are known or believed to have internalized reasoning behaviors.

description.value: Output-based efficient reasoning, which dynamically reduces reasoning steps and length during inference.
description.evidence: Output-based efficient reasoning, which dynamically reduces reasoning steps and length during inference.

description.value: Input prompts-based efficient reasoning, which seeks to enhance reasoning efficiency based on input prompt properties such as difficulty or length control.
description.evidence: Input prompts-based efficient reasoning, which seeks to enhance reasoning efficiency based on input properties such as difficulty or length control.

examples.evidence: Token-Budget [58] proposes setting a token budget in prompts to reduce unnecessary reasoning tokens.

performance_metrics.value: Performance improvements in reasoning tasks with reduced token usage.
performance_metrics.evidence: The study revealed that scaling inference-time computation alone has limitations, as no single technique consistently excels across all reasoning tasks.

results_summary.value: Efficient reasoning approaches offer significant practical benefits across various domains, reducing computational costs and enhancing decision-making.
results_summary.evidence: These advancements highlight the broad economic and societal value of efficient reasoning in LLMs.

conclusions.value: This paper provides the first structured survey of efficient reasoning in LLMs, categorizing existing approaches into three areas: model-based, reasoning output-based, and input prompts-based methods.
conclusions.evidence: This paper provides the first structured survey of efficient reasoning in LLMs, categorizing existing approaches into three areas: model-based, reasoning output-based, and input prompts-based methods.
